story.iWant: the application to work perfectly on mobile, tablet, and desktop
story.soThat: I can use it on any device

tasks.task: Audit Existing Components - Review all existing components for responsive design, identify components needing responsive updates, document current responsive state, create checklist of components to update

Update Home Page Responsiveness - Ensure hero section is responsive, make mineral prices section responsive, make featured miners section responsive (grid layout), make testimonials section responsive, update typography for mobile/tablet/desktop, test all sections at different breakpoints

Update Header Component Responsiveness - Verify hamburger menu works correctly, ensure mobile menu is fully functional, check touch targets are adequate (44x44px minimum), verify search bar is responsive, test sticky header behavior on all devices
Update Footer Component Responsiveness - Ensure footer stacks appropriately on mobile, verify links are touch-friendly, check social media icons spacing, test footer layout at all breakpoints

Implement Responsive Typography System - Define responsive typography scale, apply responsive text classes to headings, apply responsive text classes to body text, ensure line heights are appropriate, test readability at all breakpoints

Implement Responsive Grid Layouts - Create responsive grid utilities if needed, update card components to use responsive grids, ensure grids collapse to single column on mobile, test grid layouts at all breakpoints, verify spacing (gap) is appropriate

Ensure Touch-Friendly Interactions - Audit all buttons for minimum 44x44px size, audit all links for adequate touch targets, add spacing between interactive elements on mobile, remove hover-only interactions on mobile, test touch interactions on actual device if possible

Make Images and Media Responsive - Update image components to be responsive, use appropriate image sizing (max-w-full, object-fit), ensure images don't overflow containers, test video/iframe responsiveness if present, verify aspect ratios maintained

Update Forms for Mobile - Ensure form inputs stack on mobile, verify labels and inputs have adequate spacing, make form buttons touch-friendly, test form layouts at all breakpoints, verify error messages display correctly
Create Responsive Design Documentation - Document breakpoint strategy, document responsive patterns used, create responsive design guidelines, document touch target requirements

Cross-Device Testing - Test on mobile viewport (320px - 767px), test on tablet viewport (768px - 1023px), test on desktop viewport (1024px+), test on actual devices if possible, document any issues found, verify all acceptance criteria met

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Mobile Layout (320px+) - All components adapt to mobile screen sizes, navigation uses hamburger menu (already implemented in Header), text is readable without zooming, touch targets are at least 44x44px, single-column layouts for content areas, forms stack vertically on mobile

Tablet Layout (768px+) - Layout adapts to tablet screen sizes, navigation may use condensed menu or full menu based on space, multi-column layouts where appropriate (2-3 columns), touch interactions work correctly, text remains readable

Desktop Layout (1024px+) - Full desktop layout with optimal use of space, multi-column layouts (3-4 columns for grids), full navigation menu visible, hover states work correctly, text uses appropriate sizing for desktop

Responsive Typography - Text scales appropriately across breakpoints, use Tailwind responsive typography classes (text-sm, text-base, text-lg, text-xl), line heights adjust for readability, headings scale appropriately

Responsive Grid Layouts - Grid layouts adapt from multi-column to single-column on mobile, use Tailwind grid utilities (grid-cols-1, md:grid-cols-2, lg:grid-cols-3), card components stack vertically on mobile, spacing adjusts appropriately (gap-4, md:gap-6)

Touch Interactions - All interactive elements are touch-friendly (min 44x44px), buttons and links have adequate spacing on mobile, swipe gestures work where applicable, no hover-only interactions on mobile

Responsive Images and Media - Images scale appropriately without overflow, use responsive image techniques (object-fit, max-w-full), videos and iframes are responsive, aspect ratios maintained across breakpoints

Mobile Navigation - Hamburger menu works correctly (already implemented), mobile menu is accessible and easy to use, all navigation items accessible on mobile, menu closes on navigation or outside click

Form Responsiveness - Form inputs stack vertically on mobile, labels and inputs have adequate spacing, form buttons are full-width on mobile (optional, or adequate spacing), error messages display correctly on all screen sizes

Cross-Device Testing - Tested on mobile viewport (320px - 767px), tested on tablet viewport (768px - 1023px), tested on desktop viewport (1024px+), verified all breakpoints work correctly, tested actual device behavior where possible

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: Web Application Specific Requirements - Responsive Design
doc.snippet: Mobile-first approach with breakpoints: mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), desktop (1024px+). Touch-friendly interactions on mobile.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Tailwind CSS: Utility-first styling. shadcn/ui: Accessible component library. Component-based architecture with reusable UI components.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Foundation & Core Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Responsive design foundation. Next.js 15+ project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui. Core layout components (Header, Footer).

doc.title: Epics Document
doc.snippet: Use mobile-first approach with Tailwind. Implement breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px). Ensure touch targets are at least 44x44px.

doc.title: Story 1.4: Core UI Components
doc.snippet: Responsive Design: Mobile-first with Tailwind breakpoints (sm, md, lg, xl). Mobile menu uses shadcn/ui Sheet component. Header and Footer already have responsive design considerations.

artifact.reason: Header component with mobile hamburger menu already implemented using shadcn/ui Sheet. Needs verification of touch targets and responsive behavior across all breakpoints.
Footer component with responsive grid layout (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4). Needs verification of mobile stacking and touch-friendly links.
Home page component needs responsive updates for hero section, typography scaling, and button layouts across breakpoints.
Public layout wrapper that includes Header and Footer. Needs verification of responsive behavior.
Dashboard layout with navigation. Needs responsive updates for mobile/tablet views.
shadcn/ui Button component. Needs verification of touch target sizes (44x44px minimum) for mobile.
shadcn/ui Input component. Needs verification of responsive behavior and mobile-friendly sizing.
Dummy data from Story 1.9 for testing responsive layouts with realistic content (miners, listings, testimonials, events).

constraints.constraint: Mobile-first approach: Start with mobile styles, then add larger breakpoint styles using Tailwind responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:)
Tailwind CSS exclusively: Use Tailwind utility classes for responsive design, no CSS-in-JS or custom CSS files
Breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px) as per Tailwind defaults
Touch targets: Minimum 44x44px for all interactive elements on mobile devices
Responsive typography: Use Tailwind responsive text classes (text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg) for scaling
Responsive grids: Use Tailwind grid utilities (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3) for layout adaptation
Responsive images: Use max-w-full and object-fit for responsive images to prevent overflow
No hover-only interactions: All interactions must work on mobile without hover states
Component reuse: Use existing Header mobile menu (shadcn/ui Sheet component) - verify and enhance if needed
Testing: Manual verification required at mobile (320px), tablet (768px), desktop (1024px+) breakpoints. No automated tests for this story (covered in Story 1.11)

interface.name: Tailwind Responsive Classes
interface.signature: sm:, md:, lg:, xl: prefixes for responsive design

tests.standards: Manual verification testing standards: Test all components at mobile (320px), tablet (768px), desktop (1024px+) breakpoints. Verify touch targets are adequate (44x44px minimum). Test navigation on mobile (hamburger menu). Verify text is readable at all breakpoints. Test forms on mobile (stacking, spacing). No automated tests required for this story (will be covered in Story 1.11 test framework setup).

locations.location: Manual testing: Browser DevTools responsive design mode
Manual testing: Actual mobile/tablet devices if possible

ideas.test: Test mobile layout (320px-767px): Verify all components adapt correctly, navigation uses hamburger menu, text is readable, touch targets are 44x44px minimum, single-column layouts, forms stack vertically
Test tablet layout (768px-1023px): Verify layout adapts correctly, navigation menu works, multi-column layouts (2-3 columns), touch interactions work, text remains readable
Test desktop layout (1024px+): Verify full desktop layout, multi-column layouts (3-4 columns), full navigation menu visible, hover states work, text uses appropriate sizing
Test responsive typography: Verify text scales appropriately, Tailwind responsive classes work, line heights adjust, headings scale correctly
Test responsive grid layouts: Verify grids adapt from multi-column to single-column on mobile, Tailwind grid utilities work, cards stack vertically on mobile, spacing adjusts appropriately
Test touch interactions: Verify all interactive elements are touch-friendly (44x44px), buttons and links have adequate spacing, no hover-only interactions on mobile
Test responsive images and media: Verify images scale appropriately, responsive image techniques work, aspect ratios maintained
Test mobile navigation: Verify hamburger menu works correctly, mobile menu is accessible, all navigation items accessible, menu closes on navigation or outside click
Test form responsiveness: Verify form inputs stack on mobile, labels and inputs have adequate spacing, form buttons are touch-friendly, error messages display correctly
Cross-device testing: Test on mobile viewport (320px-767px), tablet viewport (768px-1023px), desktop viewport (1024px+), verify all breakpoints work correctly, test on actual devices if possible
